div.h1: TTL Expiration: Lazy vs Active Cleanup

p.em: Technical implementation guide for cache eviction strategies

div.p: Reference: Intel Optimization Manual Vol 1, Section 3.6 (Memory Alignment) State Transitions:

ol.li: Lazy: Correctness fallback. Always check TTL on READ.
Active: Background sampling N=20 keys every 100ms.
Adaptive Scan: If >25% of sample is expired, restart sweep immediately to handle high-churn TTL spikes.

div.p: Result: Bounded memory overhead with deterministic background resource cap.
T=0ms: Expiry Event Key:0xDEAD reaches TTL 0. State: EXPIRED
T=500ms: Memory Leak Entry still in index. Waste: 32B Header + Data
T=0ms: Expiry Event Key:0xCAFE reaches TTL 0.
T=100ms: Background Sweep Daemon thread wakes. EVICT(0xCAFE)
T=101ms: Request READ(0xCAFE) Result: CACHE_MISS Latency: 0.1ms (Immediate)
